bus_dispatch_cross_pool.note: F.32-1 baseline (2026-05-25). Cross-pool bus dispatch from main to io pool, 100k iters. Timed region NOW INCLUDES consumer-side drain (poll-wait on self.sub.count until iters) — F.32-4-prefetch's contribution materializes here. Pre-2026-05-25 the bench timed publisher-only and prefetch was invisible (baseline was 8.80 ms). Tolerance 0.40 because cross-thread scheduling variance + sleep granularity widen the spread. | Re-baselined 2026-05-29: the increase to ~20ms is correctness-driven — the per-arena subregion_lock (2026-05-26), the 3a9c991 bus_queue_drain race fix, and 0d1f956's per-thread-correct wire dispatch (serialize + per-subscriber deserialize to honor the single-threaded-method invariant). A flat-payload, local-only serialize-skip optimization is a deferred follow-up; the residual here is not a perf bug.

form_hashmap_false_sharing.note: F.32-1γ-v1 baseline (2026-05-25). Two-pool concurrent writers into a shared @form(hashmap, sync = lockfree, cap = 300000). 100k inserts per writer = 200k total. γ-v1 uses cell-level CAS (3-state occupancy) with NO rwlock or mutex — pure atomic ops on slot[0] + memcpy. On this 2-core / cheap-payload bench: γ-v1 is ~1.39x faster than α (serialized) and ~2.54x faster than β2-v2 (striped+rwlock); closes the gap with Go's sync.Mutex map from 1.66x to 1.18x. γ-v1 is fixed-cap (no grow) and remove-less — author sized cap to 1.5x expected entries to keep linear-probe latency bounded.

form_hashmap_walk_large.note: F.32-4a baseline (2026-05-24). 100k entries pre-populated, then full key_at/entry_at walk = O(N^2) since each call is O(N). Slow by design — exists to detect TLB pressure / cache-locality regressions. LOTUS_HUGE_PAGES=1 should reduce this once the operator reserves huge pages.

locus_instantiation.note: Re-baselined 2026-05-29. The per-arena subregion_lock correctness fix (2026-05-26, cross-pool freelist race) raised this ~91% (1.79->3.94ms). hale 856dc7d's single-thread fast-path recovered most of it (->~2.43ms); the residual is the per-arena mutex init, removable only by a lock-free subregion freelist (deferred follow-up). Measured on the dev box; the prior 1.785ms baseline was faster hardware (pre-mutex control here was ~1.99ms).
